name: Data Leak / Accidental Disclosure Self-Report
description: Internal form for employees to self-report accidental data disclosures — emailing customer data to the wrong recipient, posting credentials in a public channel, pushing secrets to a public repo, sharing a document with too-broad permissions, etc. Time-sensitive: faster reporting means we can contain impact (recall emails, rotate credentials, take down repos). High severity by default.
title: Report an Accidental Data Disclosure
intro:
Thanks for coming here. Self-reporting takes courage and it's exactly the right move — the sooner we know, the more we can contain. We routinely catch and clean up things like this; people are not punished for honest mistakes that they report.

A few things to keep in mind:

- **Speed matters more than detail.** Submit what you know now. You can update us later.
- **Don't try to fix it alone.** Tell us what's happened and what you've already tried, but don't keep deleting/recalling on your own — sometimes those actions destroy evidence we need.
- **No blame.** Mistakes happen. We're here to help limit the impact.
submit_message: Got it — this report is now in our high-priority queue and an analyst will reach out within a few minutes. Don't take any further recovery actions until we connect. Thanks for reporting fast.

fields.label: Your email
fields.help: We'll contact you within minutes to help contain this. Please stay reachable.

fields.label: What was disclosed?
fields.help: Describe what data, credentials, or document went out. Approximate volume and sensitivity is enough — we'll get the details on the call.
fields.placeholder: e.g., customer list, API keys, internal document...
fields.sample_value: Accidentally sent the Q1 customer list (about 1,200 rows including names, emails, and account tier) to john.smith@gmail.com instead of john.smith@northwindcloud.com about 20 minutes ago.

fields.label: How did it get out?
options: Email sent to wrong recipient (internal but unauthorized)
Credentials or API key posted in a public channel (Slack, Teams)
Secrets or credentials pushed to a public code repository
Document shared with too-broad permissions (public link, wrong group)
File uploaded to a personal or wrong cloud account
Data printed and left somewhere / lost
Verbally disclosed to someone who shouldn't have heard it

fields.label: Who or where did it go to?
fields.help: Email address, channel name, repo URL, person's name — whatever applies. If you don't know exactly, give your best description.

fields.label: When did it happen?
fields.help: Approximate is fine. Every minute counts for recall windows.

fields.label: How sensitive is the data?
fields.help: Your best guess. We'll confirm on the call.

fields.label: What have you already done?
fields.help: Pick anything you've already tried. If you haven't done anything yet, that's fine — leave it blank.
options: Asked the wrong recipient to delete it
Took down the public repo or made it private
Rotated the exposed credential / API key

fields.label: Anything else we should know?
fields.help: Optional. Anything about how it happened, who else knows, or context that helps us respond.
fields.sample_value: The recipient is a personal Gmail belonging to someone with the same name as our customer success rep. I don't know them. Outlook recall says 'pending' but I'm not sure if it worked.

alert_template.title: Data disclosure self-reported by {{reporter_email}}
